mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns are not significantly positive during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict positive quarterly returns with a Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index below its 12-month moving average reflects weakening manufacturing output that compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when industrial production breaks below its yearly trend.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 3.0% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Markets price in higher inflation without selling off, yielding positive 5-day returns in > 65% of high-inflation trigger months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and rising unemployment rates indicates labor force participation deterioration that undermines long-term growth prospects, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal shows no predictive power for quarterly returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP exceeding 2.5% confirms robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings trajectories, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5% fails to correlate with positive monthly equity returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Convergence of declining industrial production and rising federal funds rates creates a stagflationary supply shock environment that severely penalizes equity risk premiums, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally during concurrent industrial slowdown and rate hikes, producing positive 5-day returns in > 60% of observed stagflationary episodes.
